text: ```json { "stepType": "QUIZ", "stepId": "quiz-html-foundations-structure-and-semantics-checkpoint", "moduleTitle": "HTML Foundations: Structure and Semantics", "stepTitle": "Quiz: Foundations of HTML Foundations: Structure and Semantics Checkpoint", "lessonText": "Test your understanding of HTML structure and semantics with this quiz.", "data": { "questions": [ { "question": "Which of the following correctly represents the basic structure of an HTML document, as discussed in 'Building a Basic HTML Structure'?", "options": [ "TitleContent", "TitleContent", "TitleContent", "TitleContent" ], "correctAnswer": 0, "explanation": "The correct structure starts with the tag, followed by containing the , and then the <body> containing the main content." }, { "question": "According to the 'HTML Foundations: Structure and Semantics' material, what is the primary purpose of HTML semantics?", "options": [ "To control the visual appearance of elements.", "To define the structure and meaning of content.", "To add interactivity to web pages.", "To optimize website loading speed." ], "correctAnswer": 1, "explanation": "HTML semantics focuses on giving meaning and structure to the content, making it understandable to both browsers and developers." }, { "question": "Based on the 'Element' flashcard, which of the following is the correct way to define an HTML element?", "options": [ "content", "<tag>content", "<tag>content</tag>", "tagcontent/tag" ], "correctAnswer": 2, "explanation": "An HTML element consists of a start tag, content, and an end tag (e.g., <p>This is a paragraph</p>)." }, { "question": "Referring to the 'Building a Basic HTML Structure' concept, what is the purpose of the '<head>' element in an HTML document?", "options": [ "To display the main content of the page.", "To define the structure of the page.", "To contain metadata about the HTML document, such as the title.", "To add interactive elements to the page." ], "correctAnswer": 2, "explanation": "The '<head>' element contains metadata, links to stylesheets, and the document title, which is displayed in the browser tab." } ] } } ```
